zotero.abstractNote: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. Early diagnosis is crucial for a favorable prognosis. In addition to the gold standard of microscopic blood smear examination, rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) are used. However, these methods have limitations, particularly in resource-limited settings. Ocular changes, such as retinal hemorrhages, cotton-wool spots, and papilledema, have been described in patients with malaria, particularly in cases of cerebral malaria. These ocular findings can be detected by ophthalmoscopy, which is a non-invasive and relatively simple procedure. This review summarizes the current knowledge on ocular changes in malaria and discusses their potential as a diagnostic tool.
